title: Mastercard keeps tabs on fraud with new foundation model
optimized_headline: Mastercard's New AI Model Revolutionizes Fraud Detection Strategies
raw_excerpt:
Mastercard has developed a large tabular model (an LTM as opposed to an LLM) that’s trained on transaction data rather than text or images to help it address security and authenticity issues in digital payments. The company has trained a foundation model on billions of card transactions, with the intention of expanding to hundreds of […]

raw_body:
Mastercard has developed a large tabular model (an LTM as opposed to an LLM) that’s trained on transaction data rather than text or images to help it address security and authenticity issues in digital payments. The company has trained a foundation model on billions of card transactions, with the intention of expanding to hundreds of […]
The post Mastercard keeps tabs on fraud with new foundation model appeared first on AI News.
speedrun: Mastercard has created a new large tabular model (LTM) to combat fraud in digital payments. This model uses billions of transaction data points, focusing on security and authenticity. The goal is to enhance fraud detection and prevention as digital transactions grow. This initiative is crucial now, as online payment fraud continues to rise, impacting consumers and businesses alike.
why_it_matters: Consumers will experience more secure transactions, reducing the risk of fraud and financial loss as Mastercard enhances its fraud detection capabilities.
This move signals a shift in how financial institutions leverage AI, focusing on data-driven security solutions in an increasingly digital marketplace.

lenses.eli12: Mastercard's new model works like a security guard that learns from past incidents to spot potential fraud in real-time. By analyzing billions of transactions, it aims to keep your online purchases safe. This matters because as more people shop online, better security means less worry about fraud.
lenses.pm: For product managers, Mastercard's approach highlights the need for robust security features in payment systems. By leveraging vast transaction data, they can improve fraud detection while potentially lowering costs associated with chargebacks. This could lead to more trust in digital payment solutions.
lenses.engineer: Technically, Mastercard's large tabular model (LTM) is designed to analyze structured transaction data, differing from traditional language models. By training on billions of transactions, it aims to improve accuracy in identifying fraudulent activities. This focus on data-driven insights could set a benchmark for security models in financial technology.
